label: BMEP [bar]
notes: Brake Mean Effective Pressure.

label: Bore to Stroke Ratio
notes: Geometric ratio influencing piston area, force transmission, and mean piston speed characteristics.

label: BSFC g/kWh [g/kWh]
notes: Brake Specific Fuel Consumption (g/kWh).

label: Compression Energy Fraction(CEF) [%]
notes: Compression Energy Fraction. Portion of total heat input represented by compression work.

label: Compression/Expansion Work Ratio (Wcomp / Wexp) [%]
notes: Compression-to-expansion work ratio. Indicator of compression burden relative to expansion recovery.

label: CO₂ Intensity  [g/kWh]
notes: CO₂ intensity per unit brake energy delivered (g/kWh).

label: Compression Ratio
notes: Compression ratio. Higher CR increases end-of-compression pressure and thermodynamic severity. Reference operating range: 20–100.

label: Effective Expansion Ratio (EER)
notes: Effective Expansion Ratio determined by combustion phasing (θ_ATDC), indicating how efficiently post-combustion pressure is converted into useful expansion work and reduced exhaust loss. CR ≠ EER

label: Efficiency Brake [%]
notes: Brake thermal efficiency including mechanical losses.

label: Thermal Efficiency (Real) [%]
notes: Real indicated thermal efficiency including thermal losses.

label: Thermal Efficiency (Ideal) [%]
notes: Ideal indicated thermal efficiency.

label: Peak Piston Force (F_max) [kN]
notes: Force scales with piston area; geometry changes load even at same pressure.

label: Fuel
notes: Fuel gasoline for refernce

label: Compression Exponent n_comp
notes: Compression exponent window n = 1.05–1.20, representing near-isothermal compression behaviour within the Reference Model operating envelope. Final effective exponent is governed by calibrated control strategy in the Engineering Model.

label: Expansion Exponent n_exp
notes: Expansion Exponent

Column1: index steam_table hf j g match p2_bar steam_table pressure bar 1
index steam_table hfg j g match p2_bar steam_table pressure bar 1
index steam_table hg j g match p_theta_bar steam_table pressure bar 1 m_water_kg 1000

label: IMEP (Ideal) [bar]
notes: Ideal indicated mean effective pressure.

label: IMEP (Real)  [bar]
notes: Real indicated mean effective pressure

label: Lambda
notes: Equivalence ratio governing combustion heat release and peak temperature estimation.

label: Latent Energy Fraction [%]
notes: Latent Energy Fraction during expansion. Represents portion of cycle energy associated with phase-change contribution in expansion.

label: Loss Coolant [%]
notes: Fraction of combustion heat rejected to coolant before internal recovery. Fixed at 10% in the Reference Model.

label: Loss Unburned [%]
notes: Fraction of fuel energy not converted to useful heat due to incomplete combustion. Reference value: 0.5%.

label: Mass Water [g]
notes: Water mass per cycle determined to satisfy the sub-cooled compression constraint within the Reference Model envelope. Final water control strategy is defined and calibrated in the Engineering Model.

Column1: m_air_kg cp_air gamma_comp cv_air gamma_comp 1 cp_water

label: Mean Piston Speed [mps]
notes: Mean piston speed, function of stroke and rpm.

label: Mechanical efficiency [%]
notes: Mechanical efficiency converting indicated work to brake work. Reference value: 90%.

label: Intake Pressure P1 (derived) [bar]
notes: Derived intake pressure schedule as a function of CR to maintain the defined “No Evaporation” compression envelope in the Reference Model.
Column1: index table_p1 pressure match cr table_p1 cr 1

label: End of Compression Pressure P2 [bar]
notes: End-of-compression pressure (P2). Evaluated against the sub-cooled compression constraint.

label: Peak Combustion Pressure P3 (Ideal) [bar]
notes: Peak combustion pressure assuming ideal heat release and no losses.

label: Peak Combustion Pressure P3 (Real) [bar]
notes: Peak combustion pressure after thermal loss adjustments.

label: Pressure Exhaust [bar]
notes: Exhaust pressure at end of expansion (real cycle condition).

label: Brake Power [KW]
notes: Brake power at selected engine speed.

Column1: index steam_table pressure bar match t_water_rec_k steam_table saturation temperature k 1

label: Cooling Heat (Gross) [J]
notes: Gross cooling heat rejected for material and thermal protection prior to recovery.

label: Cooling Heat (Net) [J]
notes: Net cooling loss after recovery:Q_cool_net = Q_cool_gross − Q_rec_IHRL.

Column1: m_air_kg cv_air m_water_kg cp_steam t4_ideal_k t1_k

label: Loss Exhaust [J]
notes: Exhaust heat component after energy balance resolution.

label: Heat Friction [J]
notes: Mechanical friction loss expressed as work equivalent.

label: Heat in [J]
notes: Heat input per cycle calculated from fuel energy and equivalence ratio.

label: Recovered Heat (IHRL) [J]
notes: Cooling heat recovered through the Internal Heat Recovery Loop (IHRL) and returned as useful sensible energy.

label: Heat Unburned [J]
notes: Unburned fuel energy loss.

label: RPM
notes: Engine speed. Influences brake power, torque trends, and BSFC characteristics.

label: Temperature Input [°C]
notes: Intake temperature at start of compression. Fixed at 27 °C in the Reference Model baseline.

label: End of Compression Temp T2 [°C]
notes: End-of-compression temperature (T2). Primary indicator of thermal severity and knock margin.

label: Peak Combustion Temp T3 (Ideal) [°C]
notes: Peak combustion temperature assuming no thermal or mechanical losses.

label: Peak Combustion Temp T3 (Real) [°C]
notes: Peak combustion temperature after accounting for coolant and unburned losses.

Column1: q_in_j q_sat_j q_cool_gross_j q_ub_j cv_mix_mass m_fuel t2_k

label: Temperature Exhaust [°C]
notes: Exhaust temperature at end of expansion (real cycle).

label: Combustion phasing (ATDC)θ  [Deg]
notes: Combustion phasing (ATDC). Fixed at 5° in the Reference Model configuration.

label: Torque [Nm]
notes: Brake torque output.

label: Volume Max V1 [cc]
notes: Reference cylinder displacement fixed at 1000 cc. (Selectable in Engineering Model.)

label: Volume Min V2 [cc]
notes: Minimum cylinder volume at TDC, derived from V1 and CR.

label: Work Brake [J]
notes: Brake work per cycle after mechanical efficiency.

label: Work Compression [J]
notes: Compression work per cycle.

label: Expansion Work (Ideal) [J]
notes: Ideal expansion work per cycle.
Column1: p3_ideal_bar v_theta_m3 p4_ideal_bar v1_m3 gamma_exp 1 100000 w_theta_j

label: Expansion Work (Real) [J]
notes: Expansion work after thermal losses.
Column1: p3_real_bar v_theta_m3 p4_real_bar v1_m3 gamma_exp 1 100000 w_theta_j

label: Net Work (Ideal) [J]
notes: Net indicated work (ideal cycle).

label: Net Work(Real) [J]
notes: Net indicated work after coolant and combustion inefficiency losses.

label: Water Fuel Ratio [%]
notes: Water-to-fuel mass ratio operating window: 300–1000% (3×–10× fuel mass). This broad envelope supports thermodynamic exploration within the “No Evaporation” compression constraint. Final ratio selection and control are calibrated in the Engineering Model.

label: Water Phase in compression
notes: Water phase state under compression constraint (No Evaporation / Evaporates), evaluated against pressure–temperature boundary conditions.
Column1: if p2_bar>221 no evaporation if q_water_total <q_sen_m_water no evaporation evaporates
crank_radius_mm 1 cos theta_rad conrod_length_mm sqrt conrod_length_mm 2 crank_radius_mm sin theta_rad 2
